assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Case A creates two tasks for rhtpa-2.2: Task 1 (upstream backport) and Task 2 (downstream propagation). Both use labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] matching the expected standard labels exactly. Both are linked to TC-8020 with type: "Depend" (lines 80-86 and 142-155).

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md confirms rhtpa-2.1 is affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0) and states 'No sibling Vulnerability issues found for CVE-2026-55123 in stream rhtpa-2.1' triggering 'Case B preemptive remediation'. remediation.md Section 'Case B' (line 159) creates two preemptive tasks (Task 3 upstream backport and Task 4 downstream propagation) for stream rhtpa-2.1, described as 'Preemptive remediation tasks'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 (line 174) labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"] and Task 4 (line 251) labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Both include all four expected labels including 'security-preemptive'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 linkage (lines 235-240): type: "Related" with explicit comment '(Related, not Depend)'. Task 4 linkage (lines 303-310): type: "Related" with same explicit comment. Both preemptive tasks use 'Related' link to TC-8020, distinct from Case A tasks which use 'Depend'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 description (lines 191-194): '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Task 4 description (lines 270-273) contains identical prefix. Both reference TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE.
